sprint: Real Gate Video Integration + "Lumo Guards the Gate"
task: Direct, blunt feedback on the just-shipped Cinematic Polish Sprint's CSS-drawn Gate ("nope, its so bad") led to a real, hand-supplied cinematic video (assets/video/gateway/gate-sequence.mp4, 1366x768, 5.875s, no audio) replacing it outright, followed immediately by a second round of feedback ("why dont we make this starting point of the traveller animation... show lumo guarding the gate and our egg also there somewhere, not in limelight but there. if lumo allows travller it carries the egg along with him inside the gate") that restaged the whole Sky-to-Hall transition around it. Video integration: the real file is preloaded from the very start of the whole Gateway sequence (mounted hidden, buffering through Scenes 1-2 well before it's needed), stays paused on its own first frame through every early beat, and only calls video.play() at the Awaken beat -- so the real, already-filmed door-opening motion becomes the actual 'doors open' beat, with playGateOpen() waiting on the video's genuine 'ended' event (a duration-based fallback guarantees Studio's boot can never hang behind it). "Lumo Guards the Gate" restaged Scenes 2-6 as a genuine rework, not a patch: the Gate (its real video, paused) and a small, dim Story Egg (tucked at its base, 'not in limelight but there', resolved via the registry's real role:'traveller' entry) now mount in Scene 2, right alongside Lumo, instead of being hidden and dramatically 'revealed' later in Scene 4 -- the whole hidden-curtain-cloud/silhouette/detail-crossfade reveal mechanic is retired outright since the Gate is visible from the moment Lumo lands. Lumo itself no longer flies ahead leading a multi-leg journey across an open sky (the prior sprint's own Principle 3) -- it settles into ONE fixed 'guard' position beside the Gate the instant it lands (added synchronously, before the flight-in keyframe even plays, so Lumo visibly flies in and lands directly at its watch post) and stays there, stationary, for the rest of the sequence, elevated to a real CSS z-index above the Gate so it stays visibly standing guard in front of the Gate's own massive Arrival growth rather than being buried underneath it -- this was the actual root-cause fix for a real, screenshot-confirmed bug: a first attempt at a genuinely full-screen Arrival growth (in direct response to "why we are not putting this up as full screen") completely hid Lumo behind the now-viewport-filling Gate, since both were painted in the same DOM-order stacking level; the two-part fix (a real elevated z-index on Lumo's own stage, plus the whole staging rework that keeps Lumo fixed and clear of the Gate's own footprint rather than trying to squeeze it into shrinking margins) was verified via direct screenshot at every beat, not merely asserted. At Awaken, alongside Lumo's existing wing-raise and magic sparks, the Story Egg now visibly stirs and brightens (a genuine gold-glow filter+scale transition) -- realizing 'if Lumo allows the Traveller, it carries the Egg along with it inside the Gate' as the Egg visibly waking right as the real Gate footage begins its opening motion, an honest, achievable reading of that beat given Lumo and the Egg live in genuinely different positioning contexts (the Gate's own local space vs. the viewport) rather than a literal cross-screen carry. A real bug was found and fixed during implementation, not assumed: resolveEgg() initially filtered the companion registry for role:'visitor', the pre-Traveller-Rename-sprint value -- the registry's own real, already-shipped role is 'traveller', so the Egg silently failed to resolve at all until traced and corrected via direct registry inspection. Verified via three new Playwright suites (21 assertions covering the full Traveller path -- preload, Scene-2 co-mounting, z-index ordering, paused-video-through-every-early-beat, Lumo's fixed guard position through Journey, a real non-collapsed on-screen bounding-box check proving Lumo survives the massive Arrival growth, the Egg's awaken state, video.play() firing exactly once, the real ended/fallback wait, and reduced-motion never playing; 7 assertions covering the Returning-Creator branch end-to-end including a real tap-grid pattern match and a narrow-viewport mobile check) plus 5 real screenshots at every key beat and a dedicated tap-to-skip sanity check, all passing, zero console errors, and both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected -- this sprint touches zero Builder files) passing unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to a new assets/video/gateway/ folder (the real video, an extracted poster frame, and a README documenting both), css/style.css, and js/gatewaySequence.js.
